name: Telegram
auth_notes: The Bot API uses an API token (provided by @BotFather) passed in the URL path to authenticate requests. The full Telegram API requires a more complex registration process involving an API ID and user authorization.
api_notes: Telegram provides two primary APIs: the Bot API (simplified HTTPS interface) and the Telegram API (full MTProto-based protocol for custom clients/TDLib). Both are broad in scope, supporting messaging, media, and advanced UI primitives.
one_liner: Telegram provides APIs that allow developers to create custom messaging clients and automated bots for its platform.

evidence.claim: Telegram offers a Bot API for bots and a Telegram API for custom clients, both free of charge.
Bots are registered via @BotFather in the Telegram app, which provides an API token instantly without review or fees.
Search citation: Telegram: The Default Front Door for AI Agents (2025) | Towards AI
Search citation: Leverage the Telegram API for Sales Growth- Advantages, Use Cases, and Automation Steps - devotel

access_notes: Developers can create bots and obtain API tokens entirely for free and without approval by interacting with the @BotFather tool within the Telegram app. Full Telegram API and Bot API access is free of charge.
mcp_notes: While individual community projects (e.g., claude-code-telegram) may implement MCP servers as part of their own integrations, no official MCP server is provided by Telegram.
